Fig. 1. User-written natural language requirements may fail to accurately convey their intent. The left part shows vagueness, where the timing constraint is unspecified. The right part illustrates ambiguity, where a single requirement can have multiple interpretations.
Table 4. Human Evaluation of Interactive Transformation Methods: STL Transformation Accuracy (%) on Requirements with Different Types of Vagueness and Ambiguity, Evaluated on DeepSTL and STL-DivEn.

Table 3. Metric-based Evaluation of NL-to-STL Transformation on DeepSTL and STL-DivEn. Models Are Grouped into Three Categories: Direct Transformation with Open-source Models, Direct Transformation with Closed-source Models, and Interactive Transformation. Acc. Stands for Accuracy, and Sem. Rob. denotes semantic robustness. Bold Values Indicate the Best Results; Red Values Show ClarifySTL’s Improvements over the Previous SOTA.
